The build is expected to fail. https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-504 is created for this.

If exceptions can be thrown from different places, it would then be possible to use an exception handler to centralize in one place the generation of the response with the corresponding code and message.

Also the service layer should not manipulate the Http response, it should be done in the controller layer.

rather than passing json string, we should pass CMHandleOperation

If NCMP is returning 4XX/5XX response, then how are we handling it? Will it throw any exception or we are only returning false as response. If it just returns false, we need to find a way to handle it.

use '{"dmi-plugin":"test-dmi-service","created-cm-handles":[{"cmhandle":"node1"},{"cmhandle":"node2"}]}' instead of reading from file.

requirement is like below,
for that map should do.

"dmi-plugin" : "onap.dmi.plugin",

changed it to map , currently there is not data coming in requestbody for this. but for future created this prop.

Passing a string give generosity to ncmpclient, 
imo, client shouldn't care what kind of object needed to process the request body.

The 406 Not Acceptable is an HTTP response status code indicating that the client has requested a response using Accept- headers that the server is unable to fulfill.
This looks like a BAD_REQUEST.

We have to check this with Aditya (and examples in https://swagger.io/docs/specification/components/) and we agree Component-names in Yaml should be TitleCase e.g. CmHandles

(we'll discuss during stand-up)

json properties should be camelCase e..g cmHandles

What I understand that the structure for both created-cm-handles and updated-cm-handles is same

‘created-cm-handles” : used for initial cm handle registrations or subsequentcmhandle creations
“updated-cm-handles” : Used for updates to cmhandles. Same structure as for create handles

Can we rename it such a way so that it can used by both created and updated scenario.

In this method we are testing positive scenario but we are not validating much in then clause.
I think we should move this line in the then block, to test if this method was called with correct value as there is not way to test it. 

1 * mockNcmpRestClient.registerCmHandlesWithNcmp(json) >> new ResponseEntity<>(HttpStatus.CREATED)

We did have a discussion about not using this type of statement.But in this case we may have to use it to test it properly.

yes, in cps we still use that naming convention. For dmi plugin we have agreed to follow openapi preferred naming convention which is camel case. We will probably update cps and ncmp to follow this naming convention. https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS-390+Spike%3A+Define+and+Agree+DMI+Plugin+REST+Interface

Yes you are right, that is a bit weird. I tried changing the order but then (as this test a void method) there is not much to verify so you can leave it as it is. Thanks

the interactions in 'then' are moved before 'when' during the runtime and when it is moved it does not move the 'def' statement. 

To define to variable and use it in the interaction in then block
either we define it in given ( as we did in this test )
or we explicitly define the interaction block

then: 'register cm handle with ncmp called once and return "created" status'
            interaction {
                def expectedJson = '{"dmiPlugin":"test-dmi-service","createdCmHandles":[{"cmHandle":"node1"},{"cmHandle":"node2"}]}'
                1 * mockNcmpRestClient.registerCmHandlesWithNcmp(expectedJson) >> new ResponseEntity<>(HttpStatus.CREATED)
            }
thanks for that Renu, I always forget about the interaction-block. we can try that the next time!
